- https://github.com/charmbracelet/vhs is almost certainly the best approach
- Probably emacsclient connecting to a dedicated test emacs server will help too
- Convert to GIF with appropriate frame timing
- Ensure terminal colours are visible (use a dark theme)
- Target ~30-60 seconds, reasonable resolution

title: Show unpushed/unpulled commits in status buffer
description:
Magit shows sections for unpushed and unpulled commits in the status buffer. Add similar sections for madolt:
- 'Unpushed to origin/main (N)' section showing commits ahead of remote
- 'Unpulled from origin/main (N)' section showing commits behind remote

Requires comparing local HEAD with remote tracking branch. dolt doesn't have a built-in way to show this without fetching first, but after a fetch, comparing log ranges (dolt log origin/main..HEAD and HEAD..origin/main) would work.

Add to madolt-status-sections-hook.

title: Clean command: remove untracked tables
description:
Add a clean command (mapped to a key, maybe part of the apply group):
- dolt clean removes untracked tables from the working set
- Should prompt for confirmation with y-or-n-p listing the tables to be removed
- Could be a suffix in the dispatch transient or a standalone keybinding

This is the dolt equivalent of git clean.

title: Tag transient: create, delete, list tags
description:
Add a tag transient (t) with suffixes:
- t t: create lightweight tag (dolt tag <name> [commit])
- t k: delete tag (dolt tag -d <name>, with confirmation)
- t l: list tags (dolt tag)

Tags should be shown in log buffer ref annotations (likely already handled if dolt log includes them). The main feature is interactive tag management.

title: Cherry-pick and revert commands
description:
Add commands for cherry-pick and revert:
- A: cherry-pick transient (dolt cherry-pick <commit>)
  - A A: cherry-pick a commit (with completing-read or manual hash entry)
- Revert could be part of the same or separate:
  - dolt revert <commit> (creates a new commit that undoes changes from the specified commit)

Both commands should prompt for the commit hash/ref. Process output should be shown in the process buffer.

title: Merge transient: merge branches
description:
Add a merge transient (m) with suffixes:
- m m: merge branch into current (dolt merge <branch>, with completing-read)
- m a: abort merge (dolt merge --abort)

Should handle merge conflicts gracefully by showing conflict state in status buffer. Note: dolt merge does not support --squash or --no-ff like git does. Check dolt merge --help for available options.

title: Stash transient: create, apply, pop, drop stashes
description:
Add a stash transient (z or Z) with suffixes:
- z z: stash current changes (dolt stash)
- z p: pop most recent stash (dolt stash pop)
- z a: apply stash without removing (dolt stash apply)
- z k: drop a stash (dolt stash drop, with confirmation)
- z l: list stashes (already shown in status buffer)

The status buffer already displays stashes via madolt-insert-stashes. This adds interactive commands to create and manage them.

title: Make madolt-process buffer layout match magit-process style
description: The madolt-process buffer should more closely match magit-process layout: chevron in the left margin followed by exit code and then the command. Currently the layout differs from what magit users expect.
close_reason: Process buffer now matches magit-process layout: right-justified exit code followed by command. Tested both automated (203 tests, 199 pass) and interactive (tmux verification).

title: Show 'dolt doesn't support staging individual rows' instead of 'Nothing to stage here'
description: When trying to stage/unstage a row section, the message 'Nothing to stage here' is misleading. Instead show 'dolt doesn't support staging individual rows' (or similar for unstaging). Handle all similar scenarios where row-level operations are attempted.
close_reason: Implemented row-level error messages for stage/unstage/discard with 5 new tests

title: Fix README license - should be GPL-3.0-or-later, not MIT
description: The README states MIT license but all source files use SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-3.0-or-later. The README needs to be corrected.
close_reason: False alarm - README already correctly states GPL-3.0-or-later.

title: RET on commit in status buffer should replace buffer, not split window
description: Investigating... When pressing RET on a commit in the status buffer, it opens the revision buffer in a new window alongside the status buffer. The user expects it to replace the status buffer in the same window, similar to how they have magit configured. Need to investigate how madolt-display-buffer works vs magit's display-buffer-alist approach.
close_reason: madolt-display-buffer now delegates to magit-display-buffer when available, respecting the user's magit-display-buffer-function setting.

title: Schema changes under table headers should be indented for visual hierarchy
description: In the status buffer, when a table section (e.g. 'new table tasks') is expanded, the 'Schema changes' heading and its SQL content appear at the same indentation level as the table heading itself. They should be indented further to make it visually clear they belong under that table.
close_reason: Schema changes and row diffs under table headers in the status buffer are now indented at 6 spaces (vs 2 for the table heading) via the madolt-diff--indent dynamic variable.

title: Row diffs should distinguish between visibility level 3 (summary) and level 4 (detail)
description: In both the status view and the log view, row diffs should behave differently at magit-section visibility level 3 vs level 4. At level 3, each row change should show as a single summary line. At level 4, it should expand to show per-field details across multiple lines (as the log view already does). Currently there is no distinction between these two levels.
close_reason: Row diffs now distinguish between level 3 (summary) and level 4 (detail) in both status and log views. Status buffer now uses row-diff sections with collapsible bodies instead of plain text. Also shows schema changes inline in status buffer.

title: Commit/revision view lacks syntax colouring
description: The commit/revision buffer (shown via RET on a log entry) has lacking colouring compared to the diff views. Diff output should have proper face colouring for added/removed/modified rows, headers, and metadata.
close_reason: Fixed: madolt-log--find-entry now queries dolt directly for the specific hash instead of searching current branch history. Added madolt-diff-schema face for SQL statements in schema diff sections. Applied madolt-log-author and madolt-log-date faces to revision metadata.

title: Log view TAB expansion should match status buffer table expansion style
description: When expanding a commit in the log view with TAB, the expanded content should look similar to expanding tables in the madolt-status sections (structured diff display). Currently it shows --stat text output.
close_reason: Log view TAB expansion now shows structured per-table diffs with row-level and cell-level changes, matching the status buffer style. Replaced --stat text output with madolt-diff--insert-table-diff rendering.

title: g keybinding may prompt instead of directly refreshing
description: The spec binds g to madolt-refresh directly. The implementation inherits g from special-mode-map which calls revert-buffer. revert-buffer may prompt on non-file buffers. Magit explicitly binds g to magit-refresh. We should do the same.
close_reason: Fixed: g explicitly bound to madolt-refresh instead of inheriting revert-buffer from special-mode-map

title: Inline diff in status buffer always shows unstaged diff even for staged tables
description: madolt--wash-table-diff calls madolt-diff-insert-table which runs dolt diff -r json TABLE without --staged. Tables in the Staged changes section show their unstaged diff rather than their staged diff. Need to pass --staged when the parent section type is staged.
close_reason: Fixed: madolt--wash-table-diff now detects parent section type and passes staged flag to madolt-diff-insert-table

title: Eager diff fetching on status refresh instead of lazy on TAB
description: madolt--wash-table-diff is called inside magit-insert-section-body during refresh, meaning every table diff is fetched when the buffer refreshes, not lazily when TAB expands the section. For databases with many changed tables this causes unnecessary dolt diff calls. Should use a proper washer for deferred loading.
close_reason: Not a bug: sections are created with HIDE=t and magit-insert-section-body already defers body evaluation as a washer when section is hidden. Diffs are only fetched on TAB expansion.

title: madolt-diff--build-args reverses revision order
description: In madolt-diff.el madolt-diff--build-args, push is used to prepend rev-a then rev-b to the args list, resulting in (rev-b rev-a ...) which swaps the from..to order of the diff.
close_reason: Fixed: reversed push order in madolt-diff--build-args so rev-a comes before rev-b

title: Usability review: compare implementation against MVP spec and file issues for gaps
description:
Do a thorough usability review of the existing madolt mode:

1. Compare the implementation against specs/MVP.md to identify unimplemented features
2. Evaluate overall usability — how useful is it? What's awkward or confusing?
3. File individual beads for anything found (bugs, missing features, UX improvements)
4. Fix issues where practical

Key areas to audit:
- Status buffer layout vs spec (Remote: line, section ordering)
- Diff viewer (structured vs raw mode toggle, faces, section hierarchy)
- Log viewer (margin display, stat expansion, revision buffer)
- Commit workflow (with-editor integration, message mode, assertion)
- Keybindings completeness vs spec
- Overall polish and edge cases
close_reason: Audit complete, 11 issues filed from usability review

title: Unstaged changes should appear before staged changes in status buffer
description: In the madolt status buffer, unstaged changes currently appear after staged changes. The conventional order (matching magit) is unstaged first, then staged, so users see what still needs attention before what's already prepared for commit.
close_reason: Swapped order of madolt-insert-unstaged-changes and madolt-insert-staged-changes in madolt-status-sections-hook to match magit convention. All 190 tests pass (4 pre-existing failures unrelated).

title: Move developer info to CONTRIBUTING.md
description: Move developer-facing information (build/test instructions, interactive QA testing guide, technical notes) from AGENTS.md to a new CONTRIBUTING.md file. AGENTS.md should retain only AI agent-specific instructions.
close_reason: Moved project overview, build/test, interactive QA testing, and technical notes to CONTRIBUTING.md. AGENTS.md now references it.

title: Interactive test: refresh and buffer lifecycle
description: Test in running Emacs via emacsclient+tmux: press g to refresh status buffer, verify it updates. Make changes outside emacs (via dolt CLI), press g, verify new state is reflected. Press q to quit, verify buffer is buried. Reopen with madolt-status, verify buffer is reused.
close_reason: All 4 checks pass: g refreshes and picks up external dolt changes, q buries buffer, re-opening reuses existing buffer, multiple refresh/navigation cycles produce no post-command-hook errors.

title: Interactive test: process buffer
description: Test in running Emacs via emacsclient+tmux: perform some dolt operations (stage, refresh), then press $ to show process buffer. Verify it shows the dolt CLI commands that were run with their output and exit codes.
close_reason: All 3 checks pass: $ opens process buffer showing all dolt CLI commands with exit codes and output, buffer in madolt-process-mode, entries are magit-section process sections.

title: Interactive test: discard changes
description: Test in running Emacs via emacsclient+tmux: modify data in test db, verify unstaged changes appear, navigate to the table and press k (discard), verify confirmation prompt appears, confirm, verify changes are discarded and table disappears from unstaged section.
close_reason: Discard works: confirmation prompt appears (yes-or-no-p), answering yes discards changes, table disappears from unstaged section, data reverted in dolt. Note: use tmux send-keys for interactive prompts, not cl-letf.

title: Interactive test: dispatch menu and navigation
description: Test in running Emacs via emacsclient+tmux: press ? to show dispatch, verify all three groups visible (Transient commands, Applying changes, Essential commands). Test section navigation: n/p to move between sections, TAB to toggle sections, RET on table to visit it.
close_reason: All 4 checks pass: ? shows all 3 dispatch groups with correct keys, n navigates forward through all section types, p navigates backward, TAB toggles section collapse/expand with visual ellipsis.

title: Interactive test: log viewer
description: Test in running Emacs via emacsclient+tmux: press l l to show current branch log, verify log buffer opens with commit entries showing hash/date/author/message. Test TAB to expand a commit (shows stat). Test RET on a commit to open revision buffer with full diff.
close_reason: All 5 checks pass: l l opens log with heading 'Commits on main:', commit entries show hash/refs/date/author/message, TAB toggles stat details with visual ellipsis, RET opens revision buffer with full commit metadata and diff.

title: Interactive test: diff viewer
description: Test in running Emacs via emacsclient+tmux: modify data in test db, press d d to show working tree diff, verify diff buffer opens showing table name and row changes with cell-level detail. Test d s (staged diff). Test TAB to expand/collapse diff sections.
close_reason: All 4 checks pass: d d opens diff buffer with table sections for both modified and new tables, row changes show +/- markers with cell details, staged diff (d s) shows only staged tables, diff buffer in correct mode.

title: Interactive test: commit workflow
description: Test in running Emacs via emacsclient+tmux: stage changes, press c c to open commit transient then create commit, verify minibuffer prompts for message, provide message, verify commit succeeds and status buffer refreshes to clean state. Test c a (amend) as well.
close_reason: All 3 checks pass: commit creates commit with minibuffer message, status refreshes to clean state after commit, amend pre-fills old message and creates new commit with updated hash.

title: Interactive test: staging and unstaging tables
description: Test in running Emacs via emacsclient+tmux: create a new table or modify data so unstaged changes appear, verify unstaged section shows the table, press s to stage it, verify it moves to staged section, press u to unstage, verify it moves back. Test S (stage all) and U (unstage all).
close_reason: All 7 checks pass: unstaged changes appear correctly, s stages table at point, u unstages, S stages all, U unstages all, untracked tables shown separately, staging untracked works.

title: Interactive test: status buffer basics
description: Test in running Emacs via emacsclient+tmux: open madolt-status in test dolt db, verify header shows branch name and HEAD commit, verify recent commits section visible, verify clean status has no staged/unstaged/untracked sections.
close_reason: All 7 checks pass: header shows branch+commit, recent commits section visible with 2 entries, no staged/unstaged/untracked sections on clean db, correct mode hierarchy, correct default-directory, mode line shows Madolt Status.

title: Make ? show keybinding help like magit does
description: The current madolt-dispatch (bound to ?) only shows 5 top-level transient commands (Status, Diff, Log, Commit, Process). Magit's dispatch shows a comprehensive grid of ALL available keybindings organized by category: transient commands, applying changes (stage/unstage/discard), and essential commands (refresh, quit, toggle, visit). Update madolt-dispatch to mirror this pattern so ? gives a complete keybinding reference.
close_reason: Implemented comprehensive dispatch menu with three groups: transient commands, applying changes, and essential commands. 9 new tests added.

title: Show commit from log buffer (RET already works but improve metadata)
description:
When pressing RET on a commit in the log buffer, madolt-show-commit opens a revision buffer. The metadata section could be improved:
- Show parent commit hash(es)
- Show diff stat summary (files changed, insertions, deletions equivalent for tables)
- Show the full commit message (currently only first line)
- Better formatting of the metadata header

This is an enhancement to the existing madolt-show-commit / madolt-revision-mode.

title: Remote management: add, remove, rename remotes
description:
Add commands for managing remotes:
- M a: add remote (dolt remote add <name> <url>)
- M k: remove remote (dolt remote remove <name>, with confirmation)
- M r: rename remote (dolt remote rename <old> <new>)

The Remote: header in the status buffer already shows configured remotes. This adds interactive management commands. Could be part of the push/fetch transient or a standalone remote transient.

title: Rebase transient
description:
Add a rebase transient (r) with suffixes:
- r r: rebase current branch onto another (dolt rebase <branch>)
- r a: abort rebase (dolt rebase --abort)
- r c: continue rebase (dolt rebase --continue)

Note: dolt rebase may not support interactive mode (-i). Check dolt rebase --help for available options. This is lower priority since rebase is an advanced operation.

title: Reflog buffer
description:
Add a reflog command to view the history of ref changes:
- dolt reflog shows the history of HEAD updates
- Could be accessible from the log transient or dispatch
- Would use a dedicated buffer similar to the log buffer
- Shows hash, ref, and action for each entry

Lower priority since reflog is rarely needed in normal workflows.

title: Conflict resolution UI for merge conflicts
description:
When a merge results in conflicts, dolt stores them in special system tables. Add a UI for viewing and resolving conflicts:
- Show conflicts in the status buffer (dolt conflicts shows conflict state)
- Provide commands to resolve conflicts:
  - dolt conflicts resolve <table> marks a table's conflicts as resolved
  - dolt conflicts cat shows the conflicting rows
- May need a dedicated conflict buffer showing from/to/base values

This depends on the merge transient being implemented first.

title: SQL query interface
description:
Add an interactive SQL query interface:
- Could be invoked from a keybinding or dispatch transient suffix
- Opens a SQL input buffer (or uses minibuffer for simple queries)
- Runs dolt sql -q and displays results in a tabular buffer
- Consider using sql-mode or a custom mode for input
- Results could use a dedicated result buffer with column alignment

This is a distinctive dolt feature not found in git/magit. Lower priority since users can always use dolt sql directly in a shell.

title: Blame mode: show per-row blame annotations
description:
Add a blame command that shows which commit last modified each row of a table:
- dolt blame <table> shows revision/author/timestamp per row
- Could be accessible via a keybinding or from the dispatch transient
- Would need a dedicated blame buffer/mode with syntax highlighting
- Consider whether to show as a table or as annotated rows

This is lower priority since blame is less commonly used interactively.

title: Enumerate dolt/magit functionality and file beads for missing features
description: Systematically enumerate all dolt CLI and magit UI functionality, determine which features make sense for dolt (bearing in mind dolt doesn't support all the same things as git), and file individual beads for each missing feature that should be implemented in madolt.
close_reason: Filed 15 beads covering all missing dolt/magit features: 2 P1 (branch, push/pull/fetch), 6 P2 (stash, merge, cherry-pick/revert, tag, clean, unpushed/unpulled), 7 P3 (blame, SQL, conflict resolution, reflog, rebase, remote mgmt, commit detail improvements)

title: Untracked tables should appear first in status buffer
description: In magit, untracked files appear before unstaged changes. The madolt status buffer should follow the same convention, showing untracked tables first, then unstaged changes, then staged changes.
close_reason: Moved madolt-insert-untracked-tables before madolt-insert-unstaged-changes in madolt-status-sections-hook, matching magit convention.

title: Bulk discard triggers multiple refreshes
description: madolt-discard on the unstaged heading calls madolt-run-dolt for each child table, each triggering a full madolt-refresh. Should use madolt-call-dolt for all but the last, or batch the operation.
close_reason: Fixed: bulk discard now uses madolt-call-dolt for each table and only refreshes once at the end

title: Status buffer missing Remote: header line
description: The MVP spec shows a Remote: line in the status header (Remote: origin https://dolthub.com/...) but madolt-insert-status-header only shows the Head: line. Add remote info display.
close_reason: Added Remote: header line to status buffer. Added madolt-remotes function to parse dolt remote -v output, and madolt-branch-remote face.

title: Discard uses yes-or-no-p instead of y-or-n-p
description: The MVP spec says discard prompts with y-or-n-p but the implementation uses yes-or-no-p which requires typing yes/no instead of just y/n. More conservative but deviates from spec.
close_reason: Fixed: changed yes-or-no-p to y-or-n-p per spec

title: Log viewer uses inline text instead of right margin for author/date
description: The MVP spec describes author name and date in a right margin area (similar to magit-log, configurable via madolt-log-margin). The implementation shows them inline in the heading text.
close_reason: Log viewer now uses Emacs right-margin overlays for author/date display, matching magit-log style. Added madolt-log-margin-width and madolt-log-author-width customization options.

title: Log transient missing --graph argument
description: The MVP spec includes (-g "Graph" "--graph") in the log transient but the implementation omits it.
close_reason: Added --graph to log transient. Also strip graph decoration from dolt log output and handle refs attached without space in --graph mode.

title: Audit UX against magit for improvement opportunities
description: Audit the madolt UX against magit, which is the gold standard for this kind of interface. Look for any opportunities to improve by copying the style, design, interaction patterns, visual layout, keybindings, or anything else from magit. This includes buffer appearance, section rendering, transient menus, navigation feel, error handling UX, and any other aspects where magit sets the bar.
